docs.text: Methods Methodological pages explaining behavioral reconstructibility, topology preservation, evidence lineage, and reconstructive burden.
docs.title: Overview

docs.text: Methodological pages explaining behavioral reconstructibility, topology preservation, evidence lineage, and reconstructive burden.
docs.title: Methods

docs.text: Behavioral Reconstructibility Audit A behavioral reconstructibility audit identifies what system behavior can be safely reconstructed from documentation without inventing missing logic. It preserves: local truth evidence boundaries uncertainty branch fragmentation observability gaps
docs.title: Behavioral Reconstructibility Audit

docs.text: A behavioral reconstructibility audit identifies what system behavior can be safely reconstructed from documentation without inventing missing logic. It preserves: local truth evidence boundaries uncertainty branch fragmentation observability gaps
docs.title: Behavioral Reconstructibility Audit

docs.text: Evidence Lineage Method Evidence lineage links every finding to its source evidence. A claim without lineage is not a finding. It is either: an unsupported assumption an inference or a human arbitration overlay
docs.title: Evidence Lineage Method

docs.text: Evidence lineage links every finding to its source evidence. A claim without lineage is not a finding. It is either: an unsupported assumption an inference or a human arbitration overlay
docs.title: Evidence Lineage Method

docs.text: Reconstructive Burden Reconstructive burden is the effort required to rebuild behavior from fragmented documentation. It increases when users must cross-reference many surfaces or infer hidden dependencies.
docs.title: Reconstructive Burden

docs.text: Reconstructive burden is the effort required to rebuild behavior from fragmented documentation. It increases when users must cross-reference many surfaces or infer hidden dependencies.
docs.title: Reconstructive Burden

docs.text: Topology-Preserving Documentation Analysis Topology-preserving analysis prevents fragmented evidence from being converted into a false coherent workflow. It is useful when documentation spans: API reference UI guides support articles FAQs examples release notes
docs.title: Topology-Preserving Documentation Analysis

docs.text: Topology-preserving analysis prevents fragmented evidence from being converted into a false coherent workflow. It is useful when documentation spans: API reference UI guides support articles FAQs examples release notes
docs.title: Topology-Preserving Documentation Analysis

docs.title: Overview

docs.text: Behavioral Debt Map A behavioral debt map exposes where documentation forces users, developers, or support teams to reconstruct missing behavior.
docs.title: Behavioral Debt Map

docs.title: Behavioral Debt Map

docs.text: Documentation Topology Assessment A topology assessment identifies how documentation surfaces align, diverge, or fragment system understanding.
docs.title: Documentation Topology Assessment

docs.title: Documentation Topology Assessment

docs.text: Reconstructibility Audit Sprint A focused audit that maps where documented behavior can and cannot be reconstructed safely.
docs.title: Reconstructibility Audit Sprint
